title: Catalog Events Response
description: Response shape for GET /catalog/events on an AdCP agent that declares catalog_change_feed.supported: true. Cursor-based pagination via next_cursor + has_more. See specs/catalog-change-feed.md §API Endpoints.

events.description: Events newer than the request's cursor, in event_id (UUID v7) order. Empty array is valid (consumer is caught up but more events MAY arrive — re-poll with the same cursor or the response's next_cursor).

next_cursor.description: UUID v7 cursor for the next poll. Consumers persist this and pass as the `cursor` query parameter on the next request — unconditionally, regardless of whether events[] was empty. Behavior: when events[] is non-empty, next_cursor is the UUID of the last event in the array. When events[] is empty (consumer is caught up), next_cursor MUST echo the request's `cursor` parameter so consumers can write `cursor = response.next_cursor` without a null-check branch. Only absent when the consumer is making the initial poll (no `cursor` was sent and no events were returned).

has_more.description: Whether the agent has additional events beyond what was returned in this page. When true, the consumer SHOULD poll again immediately with next_cursor to catch up; when false, the consumer is current and can resume normal polling cadence.

retention_window_days.description: Echo of catalog_change_feed.retention_window_days from the agent's capability declaration. Surfaced on every feed response so callers don't have to re-fetch capabilities — useful when a consumer's cursor is approaching the retention edge.
